mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.5 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, supporting corporate earnings revisions and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative or flat when UNRATE drops below 4.5% in more than 60% of out-of-sample observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising commercial and industrial credit availability (RSAFS) combined with stable industrial production (INDPRO) signals a healthy demand-supply balance that precedes positive earnings surprises, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The joint momentum of RSAFS and INDPRO fails to predict S&P 500 60-day forward returns with a Rank IC exceeding 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment, declining CPIAUCSL signals successful disinflation that reduces recession risk and compresses equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL declines during periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0% do not produce positive S&P 500 20-day returns, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP reflects expanding aggregate demand that directly supports corporate revenue trajectories, consistently producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods of positive GDPC1 growth consistently produce negative or zero S&P 500 60-day forward returns, invalidating the demand-to-equity transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 150 thousand indicates robust hiring that precedes consumption spikes, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Strong PAYEMS prints fail to generate positive S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts alongside stable consumer price inflation indicates strong construction demand without inflationary pressure, signaling a soft landing that supports equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising HOUST and stable CPIAUCSL does not outperform baseline S&P 500 20-day returns in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapidly rising unemployment, federal funds rate cuts fail to stimulate equities due to dominant recession fears, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rising UNRATE combined with FEDFUNDS cuts does not produce negative S&P 500 60-day returns, or the negative predictive power vanishes in out-of-sample testing.
